rag_rules: Fix unresolved references in AGREE contracts by ensuring all identifiers are visible in the current scope or properly declared as helpers.
Use only valid AADL feature names and declared auxiliary variables in AGREE expressions to avoid 'named thing must be an expression with a type' errors.
Ensure AGREE scalar type declarations use correct types: int, real, bool; avoid package-qualified or invalid type names.
generic_rules: When generating AGREE contracts, validate that all referenced identifiers are properly declared or visible in the current scope.
Avoid introducing package-qualified identifiers in AGREE annexes unless explicitly required and present in the model.
Align helper declaration types with the types of the expressions they represent to prevent type mismatch errors.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts must be placed in valid annex scopes: component type for assumptions/guarantees, implementation for bindings.
Use assign statements only for output features or declared auxiliary variables in implementation scope.
AGREE expressions must resolve to valid types: bool for comparisons, int for integer arithmetic, real for real arithmetic.
anti_patterns: Do not reference undefined or unqualified identifiers in AGREE contracts without prior declaration or visibility in the scope.
Do not mix invalid or package-qualified scalar types in AGREE const or eq declarations.
irrelevant_topics: AADL package references and with clauses
AGREE annex placement in component types vs implementations
